beats.action: Kaelen kneels in the warm ash, his fingers tracing the thermal vent patterns beneath a cracked shale shelf.
beats.camera: wide shot, slow tilt down to Kaelen's hands on the shale

beats.action: Renna crouches behind a rusted iron rib, her hand resting tightly on her bone-shiv as she watches him.
beats.camera: medium shot, over-the-shoulder looking past Renna toward Kaelen

beats.action: Kaelen speaks calmly, then thrusts a thin metal spike into the grey crust and twists it with calculated pressure.
beats.camera: close-up on Kaelen's face, then panning down to his hands twisting the spike

dialogue.text: The condensation rate of the sulfur-spring beneath this shale suggests a pocket of potable water every twelve hours. Exactly three inches below the frost-line.

beats.action: A soft hiss escapes as water trickles into a hollow reed. Renna steps out from the shadow of the rusted rib, keeping her center of gravity low.
beats.camera: medium two-shot, Renna in foreground tracking Kaelen's throat

dialogue.text: You're digging in a dead zone, stranger. That water belongs to anyone with the teeth to keep it.

beats.action: Kaelen looks up slightly, completely detached, analyzing Renna as the water continues to trickle.

dialogue.text: It belongs to the one who understands the fluid dynamics of the shelf. Currently, that is me. I estimate you have three hours of hydration left before your motor skills begin to degrade.

beats.action: Renna demands the water. Kaelen holds the reed steady and caps it with a scraped wax-plug, refusing to pull back.
beats.camera: medium shot, low angle looking up at the two characters

dialogue.text: I've lasted three years out here without a lecture on my kidney function. Give me the reed.

beats.action: Kaelen keeps the reed secure, looking directly at Renna to present his logical proposal.

dialogue.text: An inefficient exchange. If you kill me, you obtain half a liter of water and no knowledge of how to find the next deposit. If we cooperate, my calculations will increase your daily yield by forty percent.

beats.action: Renna scoffs, her defensive posture remaining tight but her expression showing deep skepticism.

dialogue.text: Cooperate. Right. That's a word people use just before they slit your throat in your sleep.

beats.action: Kaelen states his terms indifferently, matching her gaze with absolute stillness.

dialogue.text: I have no emotional investment in your demise. It would simply decrease my own survival probability. I require local geography, faction boundaries, and resource distribution. You require sustenance and a partner who does not rely on luck.

beats.action: Renna stares at his scarred face, then slowly and deliberately relaxes her grip on the bone-shiv.
beats.camera: medium shot, panning down to her hand loosening on her weapon

dialogue.text: You speak like a machine. Or a corpse.

beats.action: Kaelen extends the reed flask. Renna hesitates, then quickly snatches it and takes a small, disciplined sip.
beats.camera: close-up on the exchange of the reed flask

dialogue.text: I am alive. And I intend to remain so.

beats.action: Renna pockets the reed and gestures toward the eastern horizon with a wary look.

dialogue.text: There's a camp two miles east. The Scrappers there don't negotiate. If you're as smart as you think you are, you'll help me avoid them.

beats.action: Kaelen rises and brushes the grey ash from his knees, his eyes already scanning the empty horizon.
beats.camera: wide shot, tracking Kaelen as he stands, establishing the vast, ash-covered wastes around them

dialogue.text: Show me the path. I will calculate the safest route.
